SET.signature: SET key value [EX seconds | PX ms | EXAT ts | PXAT ms-ts] [NX|XX] [KEEPTTL] [GET]
SET.description: Set the string value of a key with optional expiry, conditional set (NX/XX), and GET old value.

GET.description: Get the string value of key. Returns nil if key does not exist or value is not a string.

GETSET.description: Atomically set key to value and return the old value. Deprecated in favor of SET with GET option.

GETDEL.description: Get the value of key and delete the key atomically. Redis 6.2+.

GETEX.signature: GETEX key [EX seconds | PX ms | EXAT ts | PXAT ms-ts | PERSIST]
GETEX.description: Get value and optionally set or remove expiration. Redis 6.2+.

MSET.description: Set multiple key-value pairs atomically. Always succeeds.

MGET.description: Get values of all specified keys. Returns nil for non-existing keys.

MSETNX.description: Set multiple keys only if none of them exist. Atomic — all or nothing.

SETNX.description: Set key to value if key does not exist. Returns 1 if set, 0 if key exists. Use SET NX instead.

SETEX.description: Set key to value with expiration in seconds. Atomic SET + EXPIRE.

PSETEX.description: Set key to value with expiration in milliseconds.

APPEND.description: Append a value to the string at key. Creates key if it doesn't exist. Returns new string length.

STRLEN.description: Returns the length of the string stored at key. Returns 0 if key doesn't exist.

GETRANGE.description: Returns a substring of the string at key. Negative indices supported.

SETRANGE.description: Overwrite part of the string at key starting at offset.

INCR.description: Atomically increment the integer value of key by 1. Creates key with 0 if not exists.

INCRBY.description: Atomically increment the integer value of key by the given amount.

INCRBYFLOAT.description: Increment the float value of key by the given amount.

DECR.description: Atomically decrement the integer value of key by 1.

DECRBY.description: Atomically decrement the integer value of key by the given amount.

SUBSTR.description: Returns a substring. Deprecated alias for GETRANGE.

LCS.description: Find the longest common substring between two string keys. Redis 7.0+.

HSET.description: Set one or more field-value pairs in a hash. Creates hash if not exists.

HGET.description: Get the value of a field in a hash. Returns nil if field or key doesn't exist.

HMSET.description: Set multiple hash fields. Deprecated — use HSET with multiple field-value pairs.

HMGET.description: Get values of multiple hash fields. Returns nil for non-existing fields.

HGETALL.description: Get all fields and values in a hash. Returns flat list of alternating field/value.

HDEL.description: Delete one or more hash fields. Returns the number of removed fields.

HEXISTS.description: Check if a hash field exists. Returns 1 or 0.

HLEN.description: Returns the number of fields in a hash.

HKEYS.description: Returns all field names in a hash.

HVALS.description: Returns all values in a hash.

HINCRBY.description: Increment the integer value of a hash field.

HINCRBYFLOAT.description: Increment the float value of a hash field.

HSETNX.description: Set hash field only if it does not exist.

HRANDFIELD.description: Return random field(s) from a hash. Redis 6.2+.

HSCAN.description: Incrementally iterate hash fields and values.

LPUSH.description: Insert elements at the head of a list. Creates list if not exists.

RPUSH.description: Insert elements at the tail of a list.

LPOP.description: Remove and return element(s) from the head of a list.

RPOP.description: Remove and return element(s) from the tail of a list.

LLEN.description: Returns the length of a list.

LRANGE.description: Get elements from a list by index range. 0 = first, -1 = last.

LINDEX.description: Get an element by its index. Negative indices count from the end.

LSET.description: Set the element at index in a list.

LINSERT.description: Insert element before or after the pivot value.

LREM.description: Remove count occurrences of element. count>0: head to tail; count<0: tail to head; 0: all.

LTRIM.description: Trim list to the specified range.

RPOPLPUSH.description: Pop from tail of source and push to head of destination. Deprecated — use LMOVE.

LMOVE.description: Atomically pop from one list and push to another. Redis 6.2+.

BLPOP.description: Blocking pop from head. Blocks until element available or timeout.

BLMOVE.signature: BLMOVE source destination LEFT|RIGHT LEFT|RIGHT timeout

LPOS.signature: LPOS key element [RANK rank] [COUNT count] [MAXLEN len]
LPOS.description: Return position of element in list. Redis 6.0.6+.

LMPOP.description: Pop from first non-empty list. Redis 7.0+.

BLMPOP.signature: BLMPOP timeout numkeys key [key ...] LEFT|RIGHT [COUNT count]

LPUSHX.description: Push to head only if list exists.

RPUSHX.description: Push to tail only if list exists.

SADD.description: Add members to a set. Ignores existing members.

SMEMBERS.description: Get all members of a set.

SISMEMBER.description: Check if member is in set. Returns 1 or 0.

SMISMEMBER.description: Check multiple members. Returns array of 1/0. Redis 6.2+.

SCARD.description: Get the cardinality (number of elements) of a set.

SUNION.description: Return the union of multiple sets.

SINTER.description: Return the intersection of multiple sets.

SINTERCARD.description: Return cardinality of intersection without computing it. Redis 7.0+.

SDIFF.description: Return members in first set not in subsequent sets.

SMOVE.description: Atomically move member from one set to another.

ZADD.description: Add members with scores to a sorted set. NX: only add new, XX: only update, GT/LT: conditional score update.

ZREM.description: Remove members from a sorted set.

ZSCORE.description: Get the score of a member. Returns nil if not found.

ZMSCORE.description: Get scores of multiple members. Redis 6.2+.

ZRANK.description: Get the rank of a member (0-based, ascending). Redis 7.2+ supports WITHSCORE.

ZREVRANK.description: Get the rank of a member (0-based, descending).

ZCARD.description: Get the number of members in a sorted set.

ZCOUNT.description: Count members with scores in the given range. Use -inf/+inf for unbounded.

ZLEXCOUNT.description: Count members in a lex range when all scores are equal.

ZRANGE.signature: ZRANGE key min max [BYSCORE|BYLEX] [REV] [LIMIT offset count] [WITHSCORES]
ZRANGE.description: Unified range query. Replaces ZRANGEBYSCORE, ZRANGEBYLEX, ZREVRANGE since Redis 6.2.

ZRANGEBYSCORE.signature: ZRANGEBYSCORE key min max [WITHSCORES] [LIMIT offset count]
ZRANGEBYSCORE.description: Return members with scores in range. Deprecated — use ZRANGE BYSCORE.

ZRANGEBYLEX.signature: ZRANGEBYLEX key min max [LIMIT offset count]
ZRANGEBYLEX.description: Return members in a lex range. Deprecated — use ZRANGE BYLEX.

ZREVRANGE.description: Return members in reverse rank order. Deprecated — use ZRANGE REV.

ZREVRANGEBYSCORE.signature: ZREVRANGEBYSCORE key max min [WITHSCORES] [LIMIT offset count]
ZREVRANGEBYSCORE.description: Return members with scores in range, descending. Deprecated.

ZINCRBY.description: Increment the score of a member. Creates member if not exists.

ZUNIONSTORE.signature: ZUNIONSTORE dest numkeys key [key ...] [WEIGHTS w ...] [AGGREGATE SUM|MIN|MAX]
ZUNIONSTORE.description: Store union of sorted sets with optional score weighting.

ZINTERSTORE.signature: ZINTERSTORE dest numkeys key [key ...] [WEIGHTS w ...] [AGGREGATE SUM|MIN|MAX]

ZDIFFSTORE.description: Store difference of sorted sets. Redis 6.2+.

ZPOPMIN.description: Remove and return member(s) with the lowest scores.

ZPOPMAX.description: Remove and return member(s) with the highest scores.

BZPOPMIN.description: Blocking pop of member with lowest score.

BZPOPMAX.description: Blocking pop of member with highest score.

ZMPOP.description: Pop from first non-empty sorted set. Redis 7.0+.

BZMPOP.signature: BZMPOP timeout numkeys key [key ...] MIN|MAX [COUNT count]

ZRANGESTORE.signature: ZRANGESTORE dst src min max [BYSCORE|BYLEX] [REV] [LIMIT offset count]
ZRANGESTORE.description: Store specified range from sorted set. Redis 6.2+.

ZSCAN.description: Incrementally iterate sorted set members and scores.

DEL.description: Delete one or more keys synchronously. Returns count of deleted keys.

UNLINK.description: Delete keys asynchronously in a background thread. Non-blocking.

EXISTS.description: Check if key(s) exist. Returns count of existing keys.

TYPE.description: Returns the type of value at key: string, list, set, zset, hash, stream.

RENAME.description: Rename a key. Overwrites newkey if it exists.

RENAMENX.description: Rename key only if newkey does not exist.

KEYS.description: Find all keys matching a glob-style pattern. Use SCAN in production.

SCAN.description: Incrementally iterate the key space. Non-blocking alternative to KEYS.

RANDOMKEY.description: Return a random key from the current database.

EXPIRE.description: Set TTL on key in seconds. Redis 7.0+ supports conditional options.

PEXPIRE.description: Set TTL on key in milliseconds.

EXPIREAT.description: Set key to expire at a Unix timestamp (seconds).

PEXPIREAT.description: Set key to expire at a Unix timestamp (milliseconds).

TTL.description: Returns remaining time to live in seconds. -2 = key doesn't exist, -1 = no expiry.

PERSIST.description: Remove the expiration from a key, making it persistent.

EXPIRETIME.description: Returns the absolute Unix timestamp at which the key will expire. Redis 7.0+.

PEXPIRETIME.description: Returns the absolute Unix timestamp (ms) at which the key will expire. Redis 7.0+.

DUMP.description: Serialize the value stored at key in Redis-specific format.

RESTORE.signature: RESTORE key ttl serialized-value [REPLACE] [ABSTTL] [IDLETIME s] [FREQ f]
RESTORE.description: Deserialize and restore a key from DUMP output.

OBJECT ENCODING.description: Returns the internal encoding of the value: embstr, int, raw, ziplist, listpack, quicklist, hashtable, skiplist, intset.

OBJECT REFCOUNT.description: Returns the reference count of the value object.

OBJECT IDLETIME.description: Returns seconds since the key was last accessed.

OBJECT FREQ.description: Returns the logarithmic access frequency counter (requires allkeys-lfu policy).

SORT.signature: SORT key [BY pattern] [LIMIT offset count] [GET pattern ...] [ASC|DESC] [ALPHA] [STORE dest]
SORT.description: Sort elements in a list, set, or sorted set. Can dereference external keys.

SORT_RO.signature: SORT_RO key [BY pattern] [LIMIT offset count] [GET pattern ...] [ASC|DESC] [ALPHA]

COPY.description: Copy a key to another key. Redis 6.2+.

TOUCH.description: Alter the last access time of key(s).

WAIT.description: Block until writes are acknowledged by N replicas or timeout.

WAITAOF.description: Block until writes are fsynced to AOF. Redis 7.2+.

PFADD.description: Add elements to a HyperLogLog for approximate cardinality counting.

PFCOUNT.description: Return the approximate cardinality. With multiple keys, returns union cardinality.

SETBIT.description: Set or clear the bit at offset (0 or 1). Returns the original bit.

GETBIT.description: Returns the bit value at offset.

BITCOUNT.description: Count set bits (1s) in a string, optionally in a range.

BITOP.description: Perform bitwise operations between string keys.

BITPOS.description: Return position of first set/clear bit.

BITFIELD.signature: BITFIELD key [GET type offset] [SET type offset value] [INCRBY type offset increment] [OVERFLOW WRAP|SAT|FAIL]
BITFIELD.description: Perform arbitrary bitfield integer operations on strings.

BITFIELD_RO.signature: BITFIELD_RO key GET type offset [GET type offset ...]

XADD.signature: XADD key [NOMKSTREAM] [MAXLEN|MINID [=|~] threshold] *|id field value [field value ...]
XADD.description: Append a new entry to a stream. Use * for auto-generated ID.

XLEN.description: Return the number of entries in a stream.

XRANGE.description: Return entries in a range. Use - and + for min/max IDs.

XREAD.signature: XREAD [COUNT count] [BLOCK ms] STREAMS key [key ...] id [id ...]
XREAD.description: Read from one or more streams. Use $ for only new entries.

XGROUP CREATE.signature: XGROUP CREATE key group id|$ [MKSTREAM] [ENTRIESREAD n]
XGROUP CREATE.description: Create a consumer group on a stream.

XGROUP DELCONSUMER.description: Remove a consumer from a group.

XGROUP SETID.signature: XGROUP SETID key group id|$ [ENTRIESREAD n]
XGROUP SETID.description: Set the last delivered ID of a consumer group.

XREADGROUP.signature: XREADGROUP GROUP group consumer [COUNT count] [BLOCK ms] [NOACK] STREAMS key [key ...] id [id ...]
XREADGROUP.description: Read from a stream as a consumer group member. Use > for undelivered messages.

XACK.description: Acknowledge messages, removing them from the pending entries list.

XCLAIM.signature: XCLAIM key group consumer min-idle-time id [id ...] [IDLE ms] [TIME ms] [RETRYCOUNT n] [FORCE] [JUSTID]
XCLAIM.description: Transfer ownership of pending messages to another consumer.

XAUTOCLAIM.signature: XAUTOCLAIM key group consumer min-idle-time start [COUNT count] [JUSTID]

XPENDING.signature: XPENDING key group [[IDLE min-idle-time] start end count [consumer]]
XPENDING.description: Inspect the pending entries list (PEL) for a consumer group.

XINFO GROUPS.description: List consumer groups for a stream.

XINFO CONSUMERS.description: List consumers in a consumer group.

XTRIM.description: Trim stream to a maximum length or minimum ID.

XDEL.description: Delete specific entries from a stream by ID.

XSETID.description: Set the last entry ID of a stream.

SUBSCRIBE.description: Listen for messages on one or more channels.

PUBLISH.description: Post a message to a channel. Returns the number of receivers.

PSUBSCRIBE.description: Subscribe to channels matching glob-style patterns.

PUBSUB CHANNELS.description: List active channels. Optionally filter by pattern.

PUBSUB NUMPAT.description: Get the count of unique pattern subscriptions.

SPUBLISH.description: Publish to a shard channel. Redis 7.0+.

PUBSUB SHARDNUMSUB.description: Get shard channel subscriber counts. Redis 7.0+.

MULTI.description: Mark the start of a transaction block. Commands are queued until EXEC.

EXEC.description: Execute all queued commands in the transaction. Returns nil if WATCH detected a change.

DISCARD.description: Discard all queued commands in the current transaction.

WATCH.description: Watch keys for changes. If any watched key is modified before EXEC, the transaction is aborted (optimistic locking).

EVAL.description: Execute a Lua script server-side. Scripts are atomic.

EVALSHA.description: Execute a cached Lua script by SHA1 hash.

EVALRO.description: Execute Lua script in read-only mode. Redis 7.0+.

EVALSHA_RO.description: Execute cached script in read-only mode. Redis 7.0+.

SCRIPT LOAD.description: Load a Lua script into the script cache. Returns SHA1 hash.

SCRIPT EXISTS.description: Check if scripts exist in the script cache.

SCRIPT FLUSH.description: Remove all scripts from the script cache.

SCRIPT KILL.description: Kill the currently executing Lua script.

FUNCTION LOAD.description: Load a function library. Redis 7.0+. Functions persist across restarts.

FCALL.description: Call a loaded Redis function. Redis 7.0+.

FCALL_RO.description: Call a function in read-only mode. Redis 7.0+.

FUNCTION RESTORE.description: Restore functions from FUNCTION DUMP. Redis 7.0+.

GEOADD.signature: GEOADD key [NX|XX] [CH] longitude latitude member [lon lat member ...]
GEOADD.description: Add geospatial items to a sorted set.

GEODIST.description: Return distance between two geospatial members.

GEOSEARCH.signature: GEOSEARCH key FROMMEMBER member|FROMLONLAT lon lat BYRADIUS radius M|KM|FT|MI|BYBOX w h M|KM|FT|MI [ASC|DESC] [COUNT n [ANY]] [WITHCOORD] [WITHDIST] [WITHHASH]
GEOSEARCH.description: Search for geo members in radius or box. Redis 6.2+.

GEOSEARCHSTORE.signature: GEOSEARCHSTORE dest src FROMMEMBER member|FROMLONLAT lon lat BYRADIUS ...|BYBOX ... [ASC|DESC] [COUNT n] [STOREDIST]

GEORADIUS.signature: GEORADIUS key longitude latitude radius M|KM|FT|MI [WITHCOORD] [WITHDIST] [WITHHASH] [COUNT n] [ASC|DESC] [STORE key] [STOREDIST key]
GEORADIUS.description: Query by radius from coordinates. Deprecated — use GEOSEARCH.

GEORADIUSBYMEMBER.description: Query by radius from a member. Deprecated — use GEOSEARCH.

INFO.description: Get server info: server, clients, memory, persistence, stats, replication, cpu, commandstats, keyspace, etc.

CONFIG GET.description: Get configuration parameter values. Supports glob patterns.

CONFIG SET.description: Dynamically set configuration parameters without restart.

CONFIG RESETSTAT.description: Reset the statistics returned by INFO.

CONFIG REWRITE.description: Rewrite the redis.conf file with current in-memory configuration.

DBSIZE.description: Return the number of keys in the current database.

FLUSHDB.description: Remove all keys from the current database.

FLUSHALL.description: Remove all keys from all databases.

SAVE.description: Synchronously save the dataset to disk. BLOCKS the server.

LASTSAVE.description: Return the Unix timestamp of the last successful RDB save.

SHUTDOWN.description: Synchronously save (unless NOSAVE) and shut down the server.

SLOWLOG GET.description: Get entries from the slow log.

SLOWLOG LEN.description: Get the number of entries in the slow log.

TIME.description: Return the current server time as [unix-seconds, microseconds].

CLIENT ID.description: Return the ID of the current connection.

CLIENT GETNAME.description: Get the name of the current connection.

CLIENT SETNAME.description: Set the name of the current connection.

CLIENT KILL.signature: CLIENT KILL [ID id] [TYPE type] [USER username] [ADDR ip:port] [LADDR ip:port] [SKIPME yes|no]

CLIENT PAUSE.description: Pause all clients for timeout milliseconds.

CLIENT UNPAUSE.description: Resume client processing after CLIENT PAUSE.

CLIENT NO-EVICT.description: Exempt current connection from client eviction. Redis 7.0+.

CLIENT NO-TOUCH.description: Don't update key access time for current connection. Redis 7.2+.

MONITOR.description: Stream every command processed by the server in real-time. Performance impact.

PING.description: Test server connectivity. Returns PONG or echoes message.

SELECT.description: Switch to a different database (0-15 by default).

MEMORY USAGE.description: Estimate memory consumption of a key in bytes.

LATENCY LATEST.description: Return the latest latency samples for all event types.

LATENCY HISTORY.description: Return latency history for a specific event.

LATENCY GRAPH.description: Render an ASCII art latency graph for an event.

DEBUG OBJECT.description: Get debugging info about a key's internal representation.

COMMAND COUNT.description: Get total number of commands supported by the server.

COMMAND GETKEYS.description: Extract keys from a full command.

ACL LIST.description: List all users and their ACL rules.

ACL GETUSER.description: Get the ACL rules for a specific user.

ACL SETUSER.description: Create or modify a user's ACL. Rules: on/off, >password, ~pattern, +command, -command, +@category, allkeys, allcommands.

ACL WHOAMI.description: Return the username of the current connection.

ACL CAT.description: List command categories, or commands in a category.

ACL LOG.description: Show recent security events (failed auth, denied commands).

ACL SAVE.description: Save the current ACL rules to the configured ACL file.

ACL LOAD.description: Load ACL rules from the configured ACL file.

AUTH.description: Authenticate to the server. With Redis 6+ ACL, provide username.

CLUSTER INFO.description: Get cluster state: enabled, state, slots, nodes, messages.

CLUSTER NODES.description: Get the cluster topology: node IDs, addresses, flags, slots.

CLUSTER SLOTS.description: Get mapping of hash slots to nodes. Deprecated — use CLUSTER SHARDS.

CLUSTER MEET.description: Add a node to the cluster.

CLUSTER FORGET.description: Remove a node from the cluster's known nodes.

CLUSTER REPLICATE.description: Make current node a replica of the specified master.

CLUSTER FAILOVER.description: Initiate manual failover. Run on a replica to promote it.

CLUSTER RESET.description: Reset the cluster configuration of the node.

CLUSTER ADDSLOTS.description: Assign hash slots to this node.

CLUSTER DELSLOTS.description: Remove hash slots from this node.

CLUSTER SETSLOT.description: Bind, migrate, or stabilize a hash slot.

CLUSTER COUNTKEYSINSLOT.description: Count keys in a specific hash slot.

CLUSTER GETKEYSINSLOT.description: Return keys assigned to a hash slot.

CLUSTER KEYSLOT.description: Return the hash slot number for a key.

CLUSTER FLUSHSLOTS.description: Delete all slot assignments from this node.

CLUSTER SAVECONFIG.description: Save the cluster configuration to nodes.conf.

READONLY.description: Enable read queries on a cluster replica. Allows reading from replicas.

READWRITE.description: Disable READONLY mode. Replica rejects read queries.

REPLICAOF.signature: REPLICAOF host port | REPLICAOF NO ONE
REPLICAOF.description: Configure replication. REPLICAOF NO ONE promotes to master.

SLAVEOF.signature: SLAVEOF host port | SLAVEOF NO ONE

FAILOVER.description: Coordinated failover to a replica. Redis 6.2+.

MODULE LOAD.description: Load a Redis module from a shared library.

MODULE LOADEX.signature: MODULE LOADEX path [CONFIG name value ...] [ARGS arg ...]

MIGRATE.signature: MIGRATE host port key|"" db timeout [COPY] [REPLACE] [AUTH password] [AUTH2 username password] [KEYS key ...]
MIGRATE.description: Atomically transfer a key to another Redis instance.

RESET.description: Reset the connection state to default.

QUIT.description: Close the connection. Client should close after receiving OK.

HELLO.description: Switch RESP protocol version (2 or 3). Redis 6.0+.

string.description: The most basic Redis data type. A string value can be at most 512 MB. Used for caching, counters, sessions, and general key-value storage.

list.description: Ordered collection of strings. Implemented as linked list. Supports push/pop from both ends. Used for queues, stacks, and recent-items lists.

set.description: Unordered collection of unique strings. Supports add, remove, membership test, and set operations (union, intersection, difference).

zset.description: Set of unique strings ordered by a floating-point score. Supports range queries by score or rank. Used for leaderboards, rate limiters, priority queues.

hash.description: Map of field-value pairs. Memory-efficient for representing objects. Each hash can store over 4 billion field-value pairs.

stream.description: Append-only log data structure. Supports consumer groups for reliable message processing. Used for event sourcing, message queues, activity feeds.

hyperloglog.description: Probabilistic data structure for approximate cardinality counting. Uses ~12 KB regardless of set size. 0.81% standard error.

bitmap.description: String type interpreted as an array of bits. Space-efficient for storing boolean information across a large key space.

geospatial.description: Sorted set with geohash-encoded coordinates. Supports radius/box queries, distance calculations. Built on sorted sets internally.
